photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: red-brown brick walls with light gray concrete fascia and full-height dark-framed glazing
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Single-storey civic building appears maintained, with clean paved forecourt and intact glazing.
photo_observations.window_details: Long curtain-wall sections wrap the visible corner while solid brick walls punctuate the volume.
photo_observations.door_details: Entry doors are recessed within the glazed frontage.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Minimal civic roof edge with no ornamental cornice.
photo_observations.porch_details: No porch; broad forecourt apron fronts the building.
photo_observations.decorative_details: The LIBRARY lettering in the glass and the low clerestory roof form are the main visual features.
photo_observations.signage_observed: LIBRARY lettering and Toronto Public Library identification are visible.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: No major alterations obvious from these views.

facade_description: 327 Bathurst St (Sanderson Library) presents a house-form facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 1 storeys and is capped by a flat low-slope civic roof, consistent with its house-form typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a red-brown brick walls with light gray concrete fascia and full-height dark-framed glazing palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 8 window bays from lower to upper levels, using fixed, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include cornice.
